description_long: This object is a vintage-style tabletop radio receiver with a rounded rectangular wooden body and a glossy finish. The front face features a large circular tuning dial with frequency markings, two metallic control knobs, and a vertical speaker grille with multiple slats. The wood casing has a warm brown tone, and the front panel is slightly recessed, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The left and right sides are smooth and curved, with no visible controls or openings. The radio's overall design suggests it is both decorative and functional, suitable for display in urban interiors, cafes, or retro-themed environments.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio with a large tuning dial, metallic knobs, and a vertical speaker grille on the front.
description_view_0: The front-left view shows the dark speaker grille and part of the tuning dial, with the wooden casing curving around the side.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the smooth, uninterrupted wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the circular tuning dial, two metallic knobs, and the vertical speaker grille, all set in a recessed panel.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the curved wooden casing and the protruding knobs and dial from the front panel.
